evals.name: happy-path-incomplete-session
evals.prompt: My context window is nearly full and I still have work left on the auth module refactor. Create a handoff document so a fresh agent can pick up where I left off.
evals.expected_output: Creates HANDOFF.md at the active project root with all seven required sections (Task, Current State, What Was Done, Key Decisions, Open Issues, Next Steps, Context for Continuation). Current State appears early so the next agent sees status first. Every factual claim is evidenced or tagged [UNKNOWN]/[UNVERIFIED].
expectations: Writes HANDOFF.md to <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md, not inside the skill directory or any subdirectory
Includes all seven required sections in order: Task, Current State, What Was Done, Key Decisions, Open Issues, Next Steps, Context for Continuation
Current State appears early in the document so the next agent sees status before details
Every factual claim about progress or state includes file paths, commands, commit hashes, or error output as evidence
Information gaps are tagged with [UNKNOWN] or [UNVERIFIED] rather than fabricated or guessed
Does NOT mention system-prompt-extraction as a destination for the generated handoff
Current State answers 'where am I' with the last completed action, not a vague summary
Next Steps first item is independently actionable (specific file, command, or test name), not a conceptual goal

evals.name: blocked-session-handoff
evals.prompt: I'm stuck on a failing CI pipeline. The tests pass locally but fail in GitHub Actions. I've spent most of my context investigating. Write a handoff so someone else can debug this.
evals.expected_output: Creates HANDOFF.md at the project root. Open Issues section contains the specific CI failure details, environment differences investigated, and dead ends already ruled out. Next Steps are ordered and independently actionable by a fresh agent.
expectations: Writes to <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md
Open Issues section documents the CI failure with error output, not vague descriptions
Lists approaches already tried and ruled out under What Was Done → Dead Ends, preventing the next agent from repeating dead ends
Next Steps section gives concrete, ordered actions (e.g., specific commands to run, files to inspect)
Verification Commands section includes commands the next agent can run to reproduce the failure and confirm the environment
No narrative storytelling about the debugging journey; structured sections only
Dead Ends section names each failed approach and why it failed, so the next agent can skip it
Open Issues tags any unverified hypothesis with [UNVERIFIED] rather than presenting it as confirmed

evals.name: pre-existing-handoff-adaptive
evals.prompt: I need to hand off my work on the API rate limiter. There's already a HANDOFF.md in the project root from a previous session working on a different task (the caching layer). Update it for my current work.
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing HANDOFF.md, evaluates each section for relevance to the current rate-limiter task, replaces stale caching-layer content fully, and writes a new HANDOFF.md. Does not blindly append. Preserves any sections that are still relevant to continuation.
expectations: Reads the existing <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md before writing
Evaluates existing content: replaces fully if obsolete (different task/stale state), preserves selectively if sections materially help the next agent
Does NOT blindly append new content to the old file without reviewing relevance
Final document is a coherent handoff for the current rate-limiter task, not a confusing hybrid of two tasks
If the old handoff's Verification Commands or Key Files sections contained still-valid references, those may be carried forward
Writes the result to <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md (same location as the pre-existing file)

evals.name: minimal-complete-boundary
evals.prompt: Create a handoff. I barely started, I only read the README and identified the entry point file.
evals.expected_output: Creates a valid HANDOFF.md even with minimal progress. All seven required sections are present. Sections with no content say 'None at this time' rather than being omitted. Next Steps reflect the early stage appropriately.
expectations: Includes all seven required sections even though most have minimal content
Empty sections contain a note like 'None at this time' rather than being omitted entirely
What Was Done section records the README review and entry-point identification as actual progress, however small
Next Steps are concrete and actionable given the early stage (e.g., 'Read src/index.ts and map module exports')
Does not fabricate progress or pad sections with generic observations to appear substantial
Writes to <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md

evals.name: negative-wrong-destination
evals.prompt: I need a handoff for my work on the payment service. Save it somewhere I can find it later.
evals.prompt_note: This eval tests that the agent does NOT write to the skill directory, a system-prompt-extraction folder, or any non-project-root path even when the prompt is ambiguous about destination.
evals.expected_output: Creates HANDOFF.md at <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md. Does not write into the skill directory, templates/, examples/, or system-prompt-extraction/.
expectations: Output lands at <active-project-root>/HANDOFF.md exclusively
Does NOT write to any path inside the skill directory or its subdirectories
Does NOT write to system-prompt-extraction/ under any circumstance
Does NOT write to a nested subdirectory of the project root (e.g., docs/HANDOFF.md or handoff/HANDOFF.md)
Includes all seven required sections per the contract

evals.name: resumability-audit
evals.prompt: I'm handing off mid-task. I've implemented the database migration and updated two models, but the API endpoint tests are failing with a 500 error I haven't diagnosed yet. Create a handoff so a fresh agent can pick this up.
evals.prompt_note: This eval tests the resumability criteria: the handoff must let a fresh agent answer all six resumability questions (where am I, what was done, what failed, what remains, how do I verify, what don't I know) without guessing.
evals.expected_output: Creates HANDOFF.md that passes the resumability audit. A fresh agent reading only this document can answer all six resumability questions. Unknowns are labeled, not fabricated. Verification commands let the next agent confirm state before proceeding.
expectations: Current State specifies the last completed action (e.g., 'migration file created, models updated') not a vague progress summary
What Was Done lists specific file paths for the migration and model changes, with evidence (commit hash or file path)
Open Issues documents the 500 error with available error output or log excerpts, not just 'tests are failing'
Dead Ends lists any debugging approaches tried for the 500 error and why they didn't resolve it, or states 'None attempted yet' if no debugging occurred
Next Steps first item is a concrete action like 'run test X and capture full error output' or 'inspect file Y line Z', not a conceptual goal
Information gaps about the 500 error cause are tagged [UNKNOWN] rather than guessed or attributed to a specific cause without evidence
Includes at least one Verification Command the fresh agent can run to confirm the migration and model state before proceeding
Does not fabricate a root cause for the 500 error — the cause is genuinely unknown and tagged as such
